purpose: Organizing macro scenarios for dollar direction: risk-off strength (left), weak-growth weakness (middle), strong-growth strength (right).
components: Left tail: risk-off dollar strength (potentially impaired by fiscal dominance)
Middle zone: weak-growth dollar weakness (potentially supported by fiscal expansion / asymmetric smirk)
Right tail: strong-growth dollar strength (potentially capped by growth convergence)
Recalibrated range: DXY 95-108 (critiqued as not evidence-derived)
current_assessment: Descriptively useful but analytically limited. Unfalsifiable as typically deployed because three regimes exhaust all macro states. Best treated as scenario-organizing heuristic requiring ex-ante regime identification. Historical counterexamples (2017-18, Q2-Q3 2020) demonstrate predictive failure. Fiscal dollar smile concept provides novel recalibration but awaits testable prediction specification.
limitations: No real-time regime identification rule with documented hit rates produced by any agent
Three regimes exhaustively cover all macro states, making the framework unfalsifiable
Historical counterexamples demonstrate predictive failure in ~20% of year-periods
Recalibrations (e.g., DXY 95-108) not derived from the analytical framework itself
Cannot handle fiscal dominance left-tail bifurcation without ad hoc modification
